original: Beatrix Potter's "The Tale of Peter Rabbit" is published by Frederick Warne & Co. in London
1st strip ofCharlie Brown, "Li'l Folks", later "Peanuts", byCharles M. Schulzpublished in seven nationwide papers
Phileas Fogg sets out on his journey as depicted inJules Verne's "Around the World in Eighty Days"
After 5 years at seaCharles Darwinreturns to England aboard the HMS Beagle
Redwood National Park established to preserve the tallest trees on earth, by an act of US Congress with 58,000 acres (later extended)
WWII: British Royal Navy boards floundering German U-boat U-559 retrieving abandoned Enigma code machine before it sinks; 2 British sailors go down with the sub
Future world heavyweight boxing championLennox Lewis, representing Canada, wins super-heavyweight gold medal at the Seoul Olympics; beats AmericanRiddick Boweby 2nd round TKO
New York Yankees outfielderBabe Ruthhits then record 59th HR in 7-6 win over former club Boston Red Sox at Polo Grounds, NYC
Thurgood Marshallsworn in as 1st black Supreme Court Justice

Charles Stark "Doc" Draper, American Scientist and Engineer who headed the team that invented the Apollo Guidance Computer and inertial navigation technology for aircraft, space vehicles, and submarines, born in Windsor, Missouri (d. 1987)
Australian Robert 'Nat' Young puts on a dominating performance to win the World Surfing Championship at Ocean Beach, San Diego; Joyce Hoffman of Capistrano Beach, California takes women's title
Three cosmonauts return after a record 237 days in orbit

#21 University of Alabama football team beats #3 Florida, 40-39 in overtime to snap the Gators' 30-game home win streak; future Seattle Seahawks running back Shaun Alexander crosses for 4 touchdowns for the Crimson Tide
Homing pigeon averages a record 133 kph in a 1,100 km race in Australia
George Meegan, British adventurer who walked 19,019 miles from Argentina to Alaska, born in London
Hans Lippershey applies for patent for first known early telescope in the Netherlands
1st cartoon comic strip is printed in a newspaper
NY Hayden Planetarium, 4th in US, opens
New volcanic island appears off Fayal Island Azores
Green Bay Packers erupt for NFL record 49 points in the first half (35 in the second quarter) to clobber the Tampa Bay Buccaneers, 55-14 at Tampa Stadium

Pakistan cricket batsmen Inzamam-ul-Haq & Mushtaq Ahmed put on 57 runs in 8 overs for highest last-wicket partnership ever to win a Test, as the home team beats Australia by 1 wicket in the 1st Test in Karachi
Montreal Expos' pitcher Bill Stoneman no-hits the New York Mets, 7 - 0 at Parc Jarry; first MLB no-hitter ever pitched in Canada

For first time in 41 years pennant races in both MLB leagues are decided on the final day of the season; (AL) Yankees upend rival Boston Red Sox, 5-3; (NL) Dodgers beat the Phillies, 9-7 for 97-57 record; Cardinals 96-58
Bob Shaw of Chicago Cardinals sets NFL record with 5 TD receptions in 55-13 win against Baltimore Colts; Cardinals quarterback Jim Hardy tosses 6 touchdown passes
Spanky McFarland, American actor (Our Gang; The Little Rascals), born in Dallas, Texas (d. 1993)
Giuseppe Colombo, Italian mathematician and engineer who studied Mercury, known as 'the grandfather of the flyby', born in Padua, Italy (d. 1984)[1]

The pasilalinic-sympathetic compass(a contraption built to test the pseudo-scientific hypothesis that snails create a permanent telepathic link when they mate) is demonstrated but proves to be a fake.
Josephine Bakerfirst performs in Paris in La Revue Nègre at the Théâtre des Champs-Élysées
San Francisco Giants beat rival LA Dodgers, 8-7 in 4 hours 18 minutes in Game 2 NL tie-break series; longest 9-inning game in MLB history; Giants win series, 2-1
1st atomic power clock exhibited-NYC
Rod Serling's anthology series "The Twilight Zone" premieres on CBS-TV
With his team struggling at 28 for 3, Australian cricket batsman Steve Waugh smashes a brilliant 157 to guide the tourists to an innings & 99 run win over Pakistan in the 1st Test in Rawalpindi
Gelindo Bordin of Italy wins the men's marathon in 2:10:32 at the Seoul Olympics; beats world champion Douglas Wakiihuri of Kenya by 15 seconds
Steffi Grafbeats Magdalena Maleeva of Bulgaria 6-2, 6-2 in the Volkswagen Cup in Leipzig to become the youngest woman to win 500 professional tennis matches
Boston'sRay Bourquebecomes highest goal-scoring defenseman in NHL history; 386th career goal in Bruins' 3-1 win v Carolina Hurricanes moves him 1 ahead ofPaul Coffey
President Roh Moo-hyun of South Korea walks across the Military Demarcation Line into North Korea on his way to the second Inter-Korean Summit with North Korean leaderKim Jong Il
"Stan LeeDay" declared by County of Los Angeles and the City of Long Beach
Gordie Roberts, American ice hockey defenceman (Stanley Cup 1991-92, Pittsburgh Penguins; first American to 1,000 NHL games), born in Detroit, Michigan
Aziz M. Osman, Singapore-Malaysian actor and director of Malaysia's 1st sci-fi film "XX Ray", born in Singapore
Margaret Chung, Chinese-American physician who was the first known Chinese-American woman to earn a medical degree, born in Santa Barbara, California (d. 1959)
H. V. Porter, American basketball coach and administrator (created fan-shaped backboard; moulded basketball, rules films), born in Manito, Illinois (d. 1975)
Sheila Echols, American track athlete (Olympic gold 4 x 100m 1988), born in Memphis, Tennessee
The battle of Largs: Scots defeat the Norwegians on the Firth of Clyde, Scotland, returning the Western Isles to Scotland and ending 500 years of Viking invasions
French explorerJacques Cartierfirst visits the Iroquois settlement of Hochelaga (later Montreal)
FDRvisits Grand Coulee Dam construction site in Washington State
In Colorado, Nicholas Creede strikes it rich in silver during the last great silver boom of the American Old West.
Storm flood ravages Zeeland county
The Bruins beat the LA Kings, 6-5 in Boston to extend their NHL opening game unbeaten streak to 11-straight years (9-0-2)
PopeJohn Paul IIvisits New York City, and at the U.N. denounces all concentration camps and torture
Yury Glazkov, Soviet Air Force officer and cosmonaut (Soyuz 24), born in Moscow (d. 2008)
Vernor [Steffen] Vinge, American sci-fi author (Witling, True Names), born in Waukesha, Wisconsin
Don McLean, American singer and songwriter ("American Pie"; "Vincent"), born in New Rochelle, New York
Battle of Gonzales fought between Texan settlers and Mexican forces. First engagement of the Texas revolution
1st 2 aircraft collision (Milan Italy)
Amsterdam's Calvinist Churches reject Nazism
1st Netherland TV broadcast (Toverspiegel)
Guinea (French Guinea) gains independence from France (National Day)
Groundbreaking begins on Veteran's Stadium in Philadelphia
Republic Guinea forms (day of republic)
Canadian world heavyweight boxing championTommy BurnsKOs American challenger 'Fireman' Jim Flynn in 15 rounds to retain his title in Los Angeles, California
